groundings.quote: A common objection to psychological egoism, made famously by Joseph Butler, is that I must desire things other than my own welfare in order to get welfare.
G. E. Moore argued that ethical egoism is self-contradictory.

proposed_edges.quote: A common objection to psychological egoism, made famously by Joseph Butler, is that I must desire things other than my own welfare in order to get welfare.
G. E. Moore argued that ethical egoism is self-contradictory.
rules out the sort of behavior psychological egoists like to target — such as altruistic behavior or motivation by thoughts of duty alone
Ethical egoism claims that I morally ought to perform some action if and only if, and because, performing that action maximizes my self-interest.
